name: ATTOM API
what_it_does: ATTOM's property data API providing comprehensive U.S. real estate data including property details, AVM valuations, tax assessments, deed transfers, foreclosure data, school information, and neighborhood statistics for proptech and real estate agent workflows.
use_cases: Retrieving property valuations (AVM) and tax assessment data for automated underwriting or investment analysis
Pulling deed transfer history and ownership records for due diligence automation
Enriching property records with school ratings, walkability scores, and neighborhood demographics
Building foreclosure and distressed asset identification pipelines for real estate investors
Generating comparable sales (comps) data for automated property valuation models
not_for: Real-time MLS listing data — ATTOM is not a live listings feed
International property data — coverage is U.S. only
Consumer-facing applications without proper data licensing agreements
best_when: You need comprehensive, standardized U.S. property data — including AVM, tax records, and deed history — for proptech automation, investment analysis, or real estate underwriting workflows.
avoid_when: You need live MLS listings, international property coverage, or are building a consumer product without the budget for ATTOM's commercial licensing.

auth.notes: API key authentication via apikey header on all requests. API keys are provisioned through the ATTOM developer portal after account approval. Keys are account-level — no per-user scoping. Separate API keys available per data product bundle.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial with limited API calls (approximately 1,000 calls); requires registration
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter, Professional, and Enterprise tiers with custom pricing based on call volume and data products selected
pricing.notes: Pricing is tiered by call volume and data product (AVM, deed, tax, foreclosure, etc. are separate products/licenses). Enterprise contracts required for high volume or embedded use cases. Free trial is useful for evaluation but insufficient for production.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; typically 5-10 requests/second on standard plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include HTTP status codes and a message field, but error detail is often sparse. Some endpoints return 200 with empty result sets instead of 404 for not-found properties. Agents must handle empty-result responses explicitly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All primary endpoints are read-only GET requests returning property data — fully idempotent. No write operations exposed via the API.
known_agent_gotchas: Property lookups require either ATTOM ID, address components, or APN — agents must standardize address inputs before querying
Some endpoints return HTTP 200 with an empty status code in the body for not-found properties — agents must check response body, not just HTTP status
Data freshness varies by product — AVM and tax data may lag by weeks; agents should not assume real-time accuracy
Each data product (AVM, deed, tax, school, foreclosure) may require separate API key entitlements — unauthorized product access returns 401 without clear explanation
Rate limits are not consistently returned in response headers — agents must implement conservative throttling to avoid 429 errors

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth is simple but account-level — no fine-grained scoping. Separate API keys per data product provide partial segmentation. No OAuth2 or token rotation support. SOC2 certified.
